channel.title: Claimer Latest Claims
channel.description: Latest source-backed AI and technology claims with community assessment scores.

item.title: Nature published a registered report on Bluesky feeds and social feed algorithms
item.description: Evidence suggests source-backed. Attribution 94%; veracity 82%. 3 evidence entries: 2 support, 0 challenge, 1 context. Nature published a registered report on May 27, 2026 in which researchers randomly assigned 2,000 participants to use custom Bluesky feeds for eight weeks and reported that engagement-based feeds amplified intergroup, moralized, emotional, and toxic content relative to reverse-chronological feeds, while a diversified extremity algorithm reduced exposure and maintained comparable platform enjoyment. Source: Nature social feed algorithm registered report (https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-026-10536-1)
item.source: Nature social feed algorithm registered report

item.title: California issued Executive Order N-6-26 on AI workforce impacts
item.description: Evidence suggests source-backed. Attribution 96%; veracity 80%. 2 evidence entries: 1 support, 0 challenge, 1 context. California Governor Gavin Newsom issued Executive Order N-6-26 on May 21, 2026, directing state agencies to assess AI workforce impacts, review possible WARN Act updates, launch an AI employment-impact dashboard within 90 days, and prepare an AI playbook for dislocated-worker strategies. Source: Executive Order N-6-26 (https://www.gov.ca.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/05/5.21.26-AI-Workforce-EO-FINAL-SIGNED.pdf)
item.source: Executive Order N-6-26

item.title: OpenAI said it added SynthID image provenance signals and previewed a public verification tool
item.description: Evidence suggests source-backed. Attribution 96%; veracity 78%. 2 evidence entries: 1 support, 0 challenge, 1 context. OpenAI said on May 19, 2026 that it was adding Google DeepMind SynthID watermarking to images generated through ChatGPT, Codex, or the OpenAI API, alongside C2PA metadata and a public tool for checking supported OpenAI image provenance signals. Source: Advancing content provenance (https://openai.com/index/advancing-content-provenance/)
item.source: Advancing content provenance

item.title: Perplexity released Bumblebee as an open-source read-only developer endpoint scanner
item.description: Evidence suggests announced. Attribution 94%; veracity 72%. 2 evidence entries: 1 support, 0 challenge, 1 context. Perplexity released Bumblebee as an Apache-2.0 open-source, read-only scanner for macOS and Linux developer endpoints that inventories package, extension, and developer-tool metadata for software supply-chain exposure checks. Community assessment should not treat this as proof that Bumblebee prevents compromise or fully secures developer machines. Source: Perplexity Is Open-Sourcing Bumblebee (https://www.perplexity.ai/hub/blog/perplexity-is-open-sourcing-bumblebee)
item.source: Perplexity Is Open-Sourcing Bumblebee

item.title: Mistral AI announced a definitive agreement to acquire Emmi AI
item.description: Evidence suggests announced. Attribution 96%; veracity 69%. 2 evidence entries: 1 support, 0 challenge, 1 context. Mistral AI announced that it entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Emmi AI to strengthen industrial AI, physics-modeling, engineering, and manufacturing capabilities. Evidence suggests the agreement is directly attributed, while deal closing, product integration, and real-time simulation outcomes require later independent evidence. Source: Doubling Down on Science to Win Industrial AI (https://mistral.ai/news/science-to-win-industrial-ai)
item.source: Doubling Down on Science to Win Industrial AI

item.title: Meta announced Muse Spark and said it powers Meta AI experiences
item.description: Evidence suggests announced. Attribution 95%; veracity 64%. 2 evidence entries: 1 support, 0 challenge, 1 context. Meta announced Muse Spark as the first model in its Muse series and said it powers the Meta AI app and website, with staged rollout across Meta apps, AI glasses, and private API preview for select partners. Community assessment should separate Meta's announcement from independent evidence of user availability, health-adjacent behavior, shopping recommendations, API access, and capability quality. Source: Introducing Muse Spark: MSL's First Model, Purpose-Built to Prioritize People (https://about.fb.com/news/2026/04/introducing-muse-spark-meta-superintelligence-labs/)
item.source: Introducing Muse Spark: MSL's First Model, Purpose-Built to Prioritize People

item.title: Anthropic announced ten Claude agent templates for financial-services work
item.description: Evidence suggests announced. Attribution 95%; veracity 68%. 2 evidence entries: 1 support, 0 challenge, 1 context. Anthropic announced ten ready-to-run Claude agent templates for financial-services workflows, with plugin and cookbook distribution paths plus Microsoft 365 add-ins and data connectors. Evidence suggests the announcement is well attributed, while compliance, benchmark, productivity, and customer-impact claims should remain claimant-provided until independently reviewed. Source: Agents for financial services (https://www.anthropic.com/news/finance-agents)
item.source: Agents for financial services

item.title: OpenAI announced a Codex preview inside the ChatGPT mobile app
item.description: Evidence suggests announced. Attribution 96%; veracity 70%. 2 evidence entries: 1 support, 0 challenge, 1 context. OpenAI announced that Codex is now in preview in the ChatGPT mobile app so users can monitor, steer, review, and approve Codex work connected to laptops, devboxes, or remote environments. Community assessment should treat rollout, security, and enterprise availability details as separate claims needing continued review. Source: Work with Codex from anywhere (https://openai.com/index/work-with-codex-from-anywhere/)
item.source: Work with Codex from anywhere

item.title: Major Chinese AI platforms collectively restrict exam-assistance features ahead of 2026 gaokao
item.description: Evidence suggests supported. Attribution 85%; veracity 82%. 1 evidence entry: 1 support, 0 challenge, 0 context. Doubao, Alibaba's Tongyi Qianwen, Tencent's Yuanbao, Kimi, DeepSeek, and Baidu's Wenxin Yiyan have all restricted or suspended 'photo-to-solve' and academic research assistance features to prevent cheating during China's 2026 national college entrance examinations (gaokao). Source: Chinese AI Platforms Restrict Exam Features for Gaokao 2026 (https://36kr.com/p/3268412983904768)
item.source: Chinese AI Platforms Restrict Exam Features for Gaokao 2026

item.title: Pentagon remains skeptical of orbital AI data centers despite growing industry interest in space-based compute
item.description: Evidence suggests supported. Attribution 78%; veracity 65%. 1 evidence entry: 1 support, 0 challenge, 0 context. While industry interest in orbital data centers (ODCs) for AI workloads is growing due to surging compute demand, government and defense officials remain cautious. Industry sources indicate that the Pentagon has not provided significant funding, waiting for commercial entities to prove both technical feasibility and a viable business case, outside of specific NASA lunar infrastructure plans. Source: Pentagon Cautious on Orbital AI Data Centers (https://breakingdefense.com/2026/05/orbital-data-centers-ai-pentagon/)
item.source: Pentagon Cautious on Orbital AI Data Centers

item.title: Only 8% of Americans trust AI for mental health care as therapists increasingly use AI to record and transcribe sessions
item.description: Evidence suggests supported. Attribution 80%; veracity 72%. 2 evidence entries: 1 support, 1 challenge, 0 context. Therapists are increasingly using AI tools to record therapy sessions, transcribe them, and draft clinical notes for administrative efficiency. However, only 8% of Americans trust AI for mental health care according to recent polling, raising concerns about patient privacy, informed consent, and the impact on the therapeutic relationship. Source: AI in Therapy: Privacy Concerns as Clinicians Adopt Recording Tools (https://www.iowapublicradio.org/health/2026-05-26/ai-therapy-recording-privacy)
item.source: AI in Therapy: Privacy Concerns as Clinicians Adopt Recording Tools

item.title: Micron Technology stock surges on persistent AI memory shortages as HBM demand outpaces supply
item.description: Evidence suggests supported. Attribution 82%; veracity 78%. 1 evidence entry: 1 support, 0 challenge, 0 context. Micron Technology has seen significant stock growth driven by persistent memory shortages and strong demand for AI infrastructure, particularly High Bandwidth Memory (HBM). The memory sector continues to benefit from the AI boom as demand for HBM used in AI accelerators outpaces manufacturing capacity. Source: Micron's AI Memory Boom Drives Stock Gains (https://247wallst.com/technology-3/2026/05/26/micron-ai-memory-boom-stock/)
item.source: Micron's AI Memory Boom Drives Stock Gains

item.title: AIC showcases AI storage solutions integrated with NVIDIA Context Memory Platform (CMX) at COMPUTEX 2026
item.description: Evidence suggests supported. Attribution 88%; veracity 68%. 1 evidence entry: 1 support, 0 challenge, 0 context. AIC announced its participation in COMPUTEX 2026, focusing on next-generation AI infrastructure including storage solutions integrated with NVIDIA's Context Memory Platform (CMX). The solutions are designed to address memory bottlenecks in large language model inference by providing optimized storage tiers. Source: AIC at COMPUTEX 2026: AI Storage with NVIDIA CMX (https://www.eqs-news.com/en/news/aic-computex-2026-nvidia-cmx-ai-storage/)
item.source: AIC at COMPUTEX 2026: AI Storage with NVIDIA CMX

item.title: HaystackID expands AI-driven privacy and legal discovery solutions for Europe targeting EU AI Act, GDPR, and DORA compliance
item.description: Evidence suggests supported. Attribution 87%; veracity 60%. 1 evidence entry: 1 support, 0 challenge, 0 context. HaystackID announced an expansion of its AI-driven privacy and legal discovery solutions for the European market, specifically designed to help organizations navigate complex regulatory frameworks including the EU AI Act, GDPR, NIS2, and DORA. The solutions were showcased at Dublin Tech Summit 2026. Source: HaystackID Expands AI-Driven Privacy Solutions for Europe (https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/haystackid-expands-ai-driven-privacy-solutions-europe-2026.html)
item.source: HaystackID Expands AI-Driven Privacy Solutions for Europe

item.title: AIC unveils next-gen AI infrastructure for the 'Agentic AI era' with NVIDIA CMX platform integration at COMPUTEX 2026
item.description: Evidence suggests supported. Attribution 88%; veracity 62%. 1 evidence entry: 1 support, 0 challenge, 0 context. AIC announced it will showcase next-generation infrastructure designed for the Agentic AI era at COMPUTEX 2026 (June 2-5). The company plans to highlight hardware solutions including platforms integrated with NVIDIA's Context Memory Platform (CMX) aimed at overcoming memory bottlenecks in LLM inference. Source: AIC Unveils Next-Gen AI Infrastructure for the Agentic AI Era at COMPUTEX 2026 (https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/aic-unveils-next-gen-ai-infrastructure-for-the-agentic-ai-era-at-computex-2026.html)
item.source: AIC Unveils Next-Gen AI Infrastructure for the Agentic AI Era at COMPUTEX 2026

item.title: TELUS Digital reports every AI model tested was exploitable in 620,000+ adversarial attack benchmark across 34 models
item.description: Evidence suggests supported. Attribution 90%; veracity 65%. 1 evidence entry: 1 support, 0 challenge, 0 context. TELUS Digital released its GenAI Safety Model Benchmark based on over 620,000 adversarial tests across 34 leading AI models from 10 providers. The study found every model was exploitable, with attack success rates ranging from 1.3% to 93%. Reasoning models showed significantly lower vulnerability (19.9% ASR) compared to non-reasoning models (55.1%). Source: TELUS Digital GenAI Safety Model Benchmark 2026 (https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/telus-digital-genai-safety-model-benchmark-2026.html)
item.source: TELUS Digital GenAI Safety Model Benchmark 2026

item.title: Pope Leo XIV issues encyclical 'Magnifica Humanitas' calling for AI regulation and denouncing autonomous lethal weapons
item.description: Evidence suggests supported. Attribution 96%; veracity 72%. 1 evidence entry: 1 support, 0 challenge, 0 context. Pope Leo XIV signed his first encyclical on May 15, 2026 and published it on May 25, 2026, calling for AI to be 'disarmed' and stating it is 'not permissible' to entrust irreversible lethal decisions to automated systems. The document argues that 'no algorithm can make war morally acceptable.' Source: Magnifica Humanitas — Encyclical Letter of Pope Leo XIV (https://www.vatican.va/content/leo-xiv/en/encyclicals/documents/magnifica-humanitas.html)
item.source: Magnifica Humanitas — Encyclical Letter of Pope Leo XIV

item.title: Gartner predicts 40% of enterprises will demote or decommission autonomous AI agents by 2027 due to governance gaps
item.description: Evidence suggests supported. Attribution 93%; veracity 68%. 1 evidence entry: 1 support, 0 challenge, 0 context. Gartner's May 2026 press release warns that enterprises risk failure by applying uniform governance to all AI agents. They recommend a proportional approach classifying agents by autonomy level from Level 1 (Observe) to Level 4 (Act Autonomously) to manage trust boundaries and security risks. Source: Gartner Says 40% of Enterprises Will Demote or Decommission Autonomous AI Agents by 2027 (https://www.gartner.com/en/newsroom/press-releases/2026-05-26-gartner-says-40-percent-of-enterprises-will-demote-or-decommission-autonomous-ai-agents-by-2027)
item.source: Gartner Says 40% of Enterprises Will Demote or Decommission Autonomous AI Agents by 2027

item.title: Google has officially overhauled its search engine to be entirely powered by Gemini 3.5 Flash AI
item.description: Evidence suggests supported. Attribution 90%; veracity 75%. 2 evidence entries: 1 support, 1 challenge, 0 context. Google announced a full transition of its core search product to Gemini 3.5 Flash, replacing the traditional ranking algorithm with AI-generated overviews as the default experience. Source: McGill University analysis of Google's AI-powered search transition (https://www.mcgill.ca/newsroom/)
item.source: McGill University analysis of Google's AI-powered search transition

item.title: Huawei announces proprietary LogicFolding technology breakthrough, aims to produce 1.4nm chips by 2031
item.description: Evidence suggests uncertain. Attribution 82%; veracity 45%. 2 evidence entries: 1 support, 1 challenge, 0 context. Huawei claims its new LogicFolding architecture enables significant transistor density improvements, targeting 1.4nm-class chip production by 2031 despite ongoing US export restrictions on advanced semiconductor equipment. Source: Huawei unveils LogicFolding chip technology, targets 1.4nm by 2031 (https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/tech/)
item.source: Huawei unveils LogicFolding chip technology, targets 1.4nm by 2031

item.title: Airbnb CTO Ahmad Al-Dahle warns new grad hiring by tech companies has dropped 50% since 2019 due to AI automating entry-level tasks
item.description: Partially supported. Attribution 88%; veracity 65%. 2 evidence entries: 1 support, 1 challenge, 0 context. Airbnb CTO Ahmad Al-Dahle states that AI is creating a 'hollowing out' effect in tech hiring, with new graduate recruitment declining approximately 50% since 2019 as AI tools automate tasks traditionally assigned to junior engineers. Source: Airbnb CTO warns of AI hollowing out effect on entry-level tech hiring (https://dataconomy.com/)
item.source: Airbnb CTO warns of AI hollowing out effect on entry-level tech hiring

item.title: ClickUp announces 22% layoffs while restructuring around AI, offering salary bands up to $1M for high-impact AI workers
item.description: Mostly supported. Attribution 85%; veracity 72%. 2 evidence entries: 1 support, 1 challenge, 0 context. ClickUp CEO confirms a 22% workforce reduction as the company pivots to an AI-first strategy, simultaneously announcing salary bands up to $1M for key AI engineering and research roles. Source: ClickUp cuts 22% of workforce, offers million-dollar AI salaries in restructuring (https://www.indiatimes.com/technology/)
item.source: ClickUp cuts 22% of workforce, offers million-dollar AI salaries in restructuring

item.title: Market analysts warn major cloud providers' AI revenue comes substantially from startups they themselves fund, creating circular financing comparable to dot-com era patterns
item.description: Evidence mixed. Attribution 78%; veracity 60%. 2 evidence entries: 1 support, 1 challenge, 0 context. Multiple market analysts have raised concerns that a significant portion of reported AI cloud revenue growth at major providers is driven by consumption from startups funded by the providers' own venture arms, creating a circular financing dynamic reminiscent of 1990s dot-com accounting practices. Source: AI circular financing: are cloud giants inflating their own AI revenue? (https://artificiallyintimidating.com/)
item.source: AI circular financing: are cloud giants inflating their own AI revenue?

item.title: Pope Leo XIV issues first papal encyclical on AI, calling for 'disarming' of artificial intelligence
item.description: Evidence suggests supported. Attribution 92%; veracity 78%. 2 evidence entries: 1 support, 1 challenge, 0 context. The encyclical 'Magnifica Humanitas' warns against the 'culture of power' driving the AI race, calls for independent external regulation, and demands a ban on delegating lethal decisions to AI systems. Source: Encyclical Letter Magnifica Humanitas (https://www.vatican.va/content/leo-xiv/en/encyclicals/documents/20260515-magnifica-humanitas.html)
item.source: Encyclical Letter Magnifica Humanitas

item.title: OpenAI is reportedly preparing a confidential IPO filing, targeting a late-2026 public debut
item.description: Moderately supported. Attribution 85%; veracity 65%. 2 evidence entries: 1 support, 1 challenge, 0 context. Axios reported that OpenAI is working on a confidential IPO prospectus with major Wall Street banks, while OpenAI said it regularly evaluates strategic options and remains focused on execution. Source: OpenAI Prepares Confidential IPO Filing (https://www.axios.com/2026/05/20/openai-ipo-spacex-musk)
item.source: OpenAI Prepares Confidential IPO Filing
